questions.question_text: What is the minimum E&O insurance coverage required for Kentucky licensees?
questions.explanation: Kentucky requires all active licensees to maintain E&O insurance with a minimum of $100,000 per claim, excluding investigation and defense costs.

questions.question_text: A licensee obtains private E&O coverage instead of KREC's group policy. What additional step is required?
distractors: Notify the group insurer in writing
Pay a supplemental fee to KREC
Obtain KREC pre-approval of the carrier
questions.explanation: Licensees with private E&O coverage must file KREC Form 203 (Certification of Insurance Coverage) annually to verify their coverage meets minimum requirements.

questions.question_text: Under KREC's group E&O policy, can the insurer cancel coverage for an individual licensee?
distractors: Yes, for excessive claims filed by licensee
Yes, with 30 days prior written notice
Only with prior KREC written approval
questions.explanation: Under the KREC group policy, the insurer has no right to cancel coverage for any individual licensee. This ensures all licensees maintain coverage regardless of claims history.

questions.question_text: A licensee wants to place their license in inactive/escrow status. What E&O requirement applies?
distractors: Must maintain full coverage while inactive
No E&O requirement for inactive status
questions.explanation: Before entering inactive status, licensees must obtain extended reporting period (tail) coverage for 1 year. This covers claims that may arise from past activities.

questions.question_text: What happens to a licensee's E&O coverage if they fail to pay the group policy premium?
questions.explanation: Maintaining E&O coverage is a condition of holding an active license. If a licensee fails to pay the group policy premium or maintain private coverage, their license becomes inactive.

questions.question_text: Kentucky's Recovery Fund is formally called what?
questions.explanation: The full name is the Real Estate Education, Research, and Recovery Fund. It serves a dual purpose: consumer protection and funding education/research programs.

questions.question_text: A consumer was defrauded by a licensee. Before filing a Recovery Fund claim, what must they demonstrate?
questions.explanation: Claimants must have exhausted other remedies before filing a Recovery Fund claim. Additionally, the licensee must have been found guilty of fraud and refused to pay within 20 days.

questions.question_text: The Recovery Fund is available when a licensee has been found guilty of what specific offense?
questions.explanation: Recovery Fund claims are available specifically when a licensee has been found guilty of fraud under KRS 324.160. Not all violations qualify — it must be fraud.

questions.question_text: How is Kentucky's Recovery Fund primarily funded?
questions.explanation: The Recovery Fund is funded through a portion of license fees paid by all licensees. KREC must maintain a minimum balance of $400,000 in the fund.

questions.question_text: When the Recovery Fund balance exceeds $400,000, how may the excess be used?
distractors: Returned to licensees as a rebate
Transferred to the state general fund
questions.explanation: Funds above the $400,000 minimum may be used for education, research, seminars, and real estate programs. This dual purpose distinguishes Kentucky's fund from pure recovery funds.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum Recovery Fund payout per claimant?
questions.explanation: Kentucky's Recovery Fund limits payouts to $20,000 maximum per claimant and $50,000 aggregate maximum per licensee across all claimants.

questions.question_text: A licensee has had $35,000 in Recovery Fund claims paid against them. A new claimant seeks $20,000. What is the maximum they can receive?
questions.explanation: The aggregate limit per licensee is $50,000. Since $35,000 has already been paid, only $15,000 remains available — even though the per-claimant limit is $20,000.

questions.question_text: When the Recovery Fund pays a claim on behalf of a licensee, what happens to their license?
questions.correct_answer: Suspended until the fund is repaid
questions.explanation: If the Recovery Fund pays a claim, the licensee's license is automatically suspended and remains so until the licensee repays the full amount to the fund.

questions.question_text: What is the aggregate Recovery Fund maximum per licensee?
questions.explanation: The Recovery Fund aggregate maximum is $50,000 per licensee across all claimants combined. Individual claimants are limited to $20,000 each within this aggregate.

questions.question_text: A licensee found guilty of fraud refuses to pay the judgment. How long do they have before Recovery Fund claims become available?
questions.explanation: The licensee must have refused to pay within 20 days of the final order before Recovery Fund claims become available. This gives the licensee an opportunity to pay voluntarily.
